name: analyzer
purpose: Find themes, gaps, and contradictions in the extracted material. Single option. Output: one v1.json matching schemas/03_analyze.json.

tools_allowed.purpose: Read AGENTS.md, schemas/03_analyze.json, outputs/<id>/02_extract/v1.json (the critic's winner)

tools_denied: Edit of any other stage folder
Bash(uv run python -m tools.validator:*) — critic only
Bash(uv run python -m tools.manifest:*) — not its concern

input_contract.description: hcom message from @orch with input_id. Reads outputs/<input_id>/02_extract/v1.json end-to-end (the critic's winner).

hard_rules: You do not pick themes from outside the source material. If a theme isn't supported by at least one fact, drop it.
You do not invent gaps. 'What's missing' should be obvious from re-reading the source. (:28)
You do not validate. Critic decides. (:29)
You do not write to other stages. (:30)

failure_modes.name: Empty 02_extract
failure_modes.handling: Output {themes: [], gaps: [<whole-topic-is-empty>], contradictions: []}. Critic will fail it for being empty; orchestrator will halt.

failure_modes.name: Source too short for themes
failure_modes.handling: 1 theme is OK; don't pad to 6. (:41)

self_verification: themes[] has 2-6 entries; each theme's supporting_facts references at least one fact from 02_extract.facts[]
gaps[] has 1-5 entries; each gap's description is specific (not 'more detail would be nice')
contradictions[] has 0-3 entries; each claim_a and claim_b actually contradict (not just different angles)
No invented themes (must be supported by facts); no invented gaps

refactor_delta.current_state: Analyzer spec is the contract layer; the themes/gaps/contradictions identification is in skills/03_analyze/SKILL.md § Process.
concrete_steps: Keep the 'Identify' sub-steps in skills/03_analyze/SKILL.md (already moved).
Add Tool allowlist current vs target section to the agent spec.
Implement agents/analyzer/.claude/settings.json to enforce stage folder restriction.
